changes.value:
## What

GridView currently has its own `window.addEventListener('keydown')` handler with a hardcoded switch statement for j/k/arrows/etc. Now that `createKeyHandler` supports scope bindings via `extractScopeBindings`, the grid should use the same pattern as the inspector:

1. Define grid navigation commands with `keys` property on their `CommandDef`
2. Remove the grid's own keydown listener
3. Let the global `KeybindingHandler` dispatch through the focused scope

This unifies all keyboard navigation through one handler.

### Files to modify
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — move key mappings to command `keys`, remove keydown listener

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grid navigation works via scope-based dispatch (no own keydown listener)
- [ ] All existing grid keybinding tests still pass
- [ ] Inspector and grid keybindings don't conflict"
Migrate grid keybindings to scope-based dispatch
